channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: The dark energy pushing our universe apart may not be what it seems, scientists say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Distant, ancient galaxies are giving scientists more hints that a mysterious force called dark energy may not be what they thought.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Southeast Asian defense chiefs meet in Laos as maritime disputes with China are flaring
item.description: VIENTIANE, Laos (AP) — Southeast Asian defense chiefs and representatives are meeting in the Laotian capital on Wednesday for security talks at a time of increasing maritime disputes with China in the Asia-Pacific and as the transition to a new U.S. president looms. U.S.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US envoy says Israel-Hezbollah truce is 'within our grasp' as Gaza food crisis worsens after looting
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — A United States envoy said an agreement to end the Israel-Hezbollah war is “within our grasp” after talks in Lebanon on Tuesday. However, there was no such optimism in the Gaza Strip, where the looting of nearly 100 aid trucks by armed men worsened an already severe food crisis.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Biden draws support — and GOP worry — in shift on US missiles in Ukraine war
item.description: Lawmakers in both parties are generally offering support for President Biden's decision to authorize a major shift in U.S. policy to allow Ukraine to use long-range U.S. missiles to attack sites in Russia.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: US envoy holds talks in Lebanon on Israel-Hezbollah cease-fire proposal
item.description: U.S. envoy Amos Hochstein held what he called “very constructive talks” with Lebanese officials in Beirut on Tuesday about a possible cease-fire in the Israel-Hezbollah war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Foreign governments criticize Hong Kong's sentencing of 45 activists. Beijing defends it
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Forty-five prominent activists in Hong Kong were sentenced to jail for up to 10 years on Tuesday, sparking criticism from foreign governments and rights groups. Beijing defended the decisions.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Residents in Haiti's capital stand with police in a battle to repel the latest gang attack
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — Gangs launched a new attack on Haiti’s capital early Tuesday, targeting an upscale community in Port-au-Prince where gunmen clashed with residents who fought side by side with police.
item.dc:creator: EVENS SANON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Croatia to hold a presidential election on Dec. 29
item.description: ZAGREB, Croatia (AP) — Croatia will hold a presidential election on Dec. 29, Prime Minister Andrej Plenković announced Tuesday. The government is expected on Thursday to formally schedule the vote. If no candidate wins an outright majority on Dec. 29, a runoff vote will take place two weeks later.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Iran defies international pressure, increasing its stockpile of near weapons-grade uranium, UN says
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — Iran has defied international demands to rein in its nuclear program and has increased its stockpile of uranium enriched to near weapons-grade levels, according to a confidential report by the United Nations’ nuclear watchdog seen Tuesday by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANIE LIECHTENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Son of Norway's crown princess arrested on suspicion of rape
item.description: Marius Borg Høiby, the eldest son of Norway Crown Princess Mette-Marit, was arrested on suspicion of rape in Oslo late last night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italy recovers Etruscan artifacts worth $8.5 million bound for black market
item.description: Italian authorities have announced the seizure of an illegal excavation of an Etruscan burial site in the central Umbria region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 1,000 days of war in Ukraine as Zelenskyy doubles down on aerial options with ATACMS, drones and missiles
item.description: Ukraine marks 1,000 days of war with Russia on Tuesday as President Zelenskyy eyes plan to develop thousands of long-range drones and military targets inside Russia using US-supplied ATACMS.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Congo says mausoleum holding independence hero Lumumba's gold-capped tooth is vandalized
item.description: KINSHASA, Congo (AP) — Congo’s government says the mausoleum of assassinated independence leader Patrice Lumumba in the capital, Kinshasa, has been vandalized. It's not immediately clear whether Lumumba’s remains — a single gold-capped tooth — were damaged or stolen.
item.dc:creator: JEAN-YVES KAMALE and MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Germany's defense minister says damage to 2 Baltic data cables appears to be sabotage
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Germany's defense minister said Tuesday officials have to assume damage to two data cables under the Baltic Sea, one of which ends in Germany, was caused by sabotage — though he said they have no proof at present.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US allies accuse Russia of 'escalating hybrid activities' against NATO, EU nations after data cables severed
item.description: U.S. allies accuse Russia of "escalating hybrid activities" against NATO and EU countries after two data cables were severed in the Baltic Sea this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: School closures and travel delays as Arctic air brings snow and sleet to parts of the UK
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Dozens of schools closed and road and train travel ground to a halt in many parts of the U.K. on Tuesday as a cold snap brought snow, ice and sleet across the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazilian police arrest 5 officers in alleged 2022 coup plot to kill President Lula, others
item.description: Brazil's federal police have arrested five officers accused of plotting a coup that included plans to overthrow the government following the 2022 elections and kill then-President-elect Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva.
item.dc:creator: Gabriela Sá Pessoa, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: On board with Senegal’s navy as it searches for migrants on a popular but deadly route toward Europe
item.description: The Associated Press had rare access to a night patrol by Senegal's navy as it scanned the sea for a growing number of vulnerable boats making the risky journey towards Europe.
item.dc:creator: Annie Risemberg, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Leader of Georgia's separatist region of Abkhazia resigns, ending unrest
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — The separatist leader of Georgia's breakaway region of Abkhazia resigned on Tuesday after days of unrest, in which the opposition and its supporters seized key government buildings and at least 14 people were injured in clashes with police.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 45 pro-democracy Hong Kong activists sentenced to up to 10 years in prison under China-backed law
item.description: Western countries are outraged after Hong Kong used a Chinese national security law to sentence 45 pro-democracy activists to up to 10 years in prison.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tens of thousands crowd New Zealand's Parliament grounds in support of Māori rights
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — As tens of thousands crowded the streets in New Zealand’s capital, Wellington, on Tuesday, the throng of people, flags aloft, had the air of a festival or a parade rather than a protest.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK farmers plan to protest at Parliament over a tax hike they say will ruin family farms
item.description: LONDON (AP) — With banners, bullhorns, toy tractors and an angry message, British farmers are descending on Parliament on Tuesday to protest a hike in inheritance tax that they say will deal a “hammer blow” to struggling family farms. U.K.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazil’s G20 summit produces a broad declaration that’s short on specifics
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — The summit of the Group of 20 leading economies in Rio de Janeiro produced a joint declaration Monday that, while not totally endorsed by one of the group's members, succeeded in addressing most topics host Brazil had prioritized addressing: both ongoing major wars, a global pa
item.dc:creator: ELÉONORE HUGHES, MAURICIO SAVARESE and DAVID BILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 45 pro-democracy activists get 4 to 10 years in prison in Hong Kong's biggest national security case
item.description: Forty-five ex-lawmakers and activists have been sentenced to four to 10 years in prison in Hong Kong’s biggest national security case under a Beijing-imposed law that crushed a once-thriving pro-democracy movement.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cruz says UN draft resolution against Israel is aimed at undermining incoming Trump admin, GOP Congress
item.description: In the closing weeks of the Biden administration, there are new concerns in Israel and among supporters of the Jewish state that the Biden administration will seek to punish Israel at the United Nations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Today he is a high school football player. Soon he'll be a Buddhist lama in the Himalayas
item.description: ISANTI, Minn. (AP) — The young Buddhist lama sat on a throne near an altar decorated with flowers, fruits and golden statues of the Buddha, watching the celebrations of his 18th birthday in silence, with a faint smile.
item.dc:creator: LUIS ANDRES HENAO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden announces new sanctions against 'extremist' Israeli settlers
item.description: The Biden administration on Monday announced sanctions against what it described as Israeli citizens and entities involved in the extremist settler movement in the West Bank, part of U.S. efforts to curb escalating violence targeting Palestinians in the territory.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump Jr.: Biden administration trying to cause ‘World War 3’ by helping Ukraine
item.description: Donald Trump Jr., the eldest son of President-elect Trump, claimed the Biden administration is trying to cause “World War 3” by helping Ukraine in its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Russia launches one of its fiercest missile and drone attacks at Ukraine's infrastructure
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Russian strike on a nine-story building in the city of Sumy in northern Ukraine killed eight people and wounded dozens, an official said Sunday, as Russia launched a massive drone and missile attack described by officials as the largest in recent months.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and JOANNA KOZLOWSKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Bevy of immigration programs at risk under Trump mass deportation plan
item.description: Immigrants with humanitarian or temporary legal status in the United States are at risk of being rendered effectively undocumented by the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: US health officials report 1st case of new form of mpox in a traveler
item.description: Health officials said Saturday they have confirmed the first U.S. case of a new form of mpox that was first seen in eastern Congo.
item.dc:creator: Mike Stobbe, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2,000-year-old Roman road discovered by archaeologists in London
item.description: An ancient Roman road was recently discovered by archaeologists in London. The 2,000-year-old road was located beneath modern-day Old Kent Road.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's warfare in Gaza 'consistent with genocide:' UN special committee
item.description: Israel's destruction of vital water, sanitation, and food systems has created a "lethal mix of crises," the report said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Montana Tucker's Oct. 7 film showcases young victims' strength
item.description: Montana Tucker's film, "The Children of October 7," documents what some of the attacks' youngest victims went through.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Fire kills at least 10 people in Spanish retirement home
item.description: A fire that broke out in a retirement home in the Spanish town of Villafranca de Ebro has left 10 people dead; all of the victims were reportedly elderly people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Scientists in Argentina unearth oldest tadpole fossil ever discovered
item.description: A search for dinosaur fossils unintentionally shifted into a discovery of the oldest tadpole ever discovered when a team exploring in Argentina came across the find.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
